stemHtml: What is one of the advantages of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) in flight operations?

options.a: SOPs for emergency situations must be followed by the crew, as they help to ensure a safe outcome, though flight crew can modify SOPs for normal situations, if so agreed during the pre-flight briefing.
options.b: A well-trained pilot will follow SOPs without the need to employ cognitive resources in the decision-making process, thereby maintaining the workload at an optimum level.
options.c: SOPs are an effective aid to decision-making for the flight crew, reducing their workload and reducing the probability of taking poor decisions and making errors.
options.d: SOPs are presented in written form as checklists and manuals, so pilots do NOT need to store in long-term memory the correct procedure for a given situation.
